title: State Department UAP Cable 3, Tbilisi, Georgia, October 30, 2001
summary: On October 28-29, there was an incident alleged by the Georgian Foreign Ministry that Russian aircraft had violated Georgian airspace and bombed areas of the Kodori Gorge.  Russians denied any of the claims and said that it could have been UFOs.  Cable authors note that Russians typically engage in the “bold lie” when they wish to conceal actions.
csv_raw_title: State Department UAP Cable 3, Tbilisi, Georgia, October 30, 2001

audience.tldr: State Department diplomatic cable from an overseas diplomatic post documenting a UAP encounter (10/28/2001-10/29/2001) over Georgia.
what_we_dont: No kinematic data (speed, altitude, maneuver) is attached.
No official conclusion has been issued classifying the object as conventional.
Independent corroboration from non-government sources has not been confirmed.
audience.mundane_candidate: A conventional explanation is technically possible but has not been demonstrated.
audience.score_sentence: Anomalousness Index 50/100. Top drivers: corroboration (single_witness_instrument), witness credibility (civilian_credentialed).
